name: codex_shell_smoke
description: Small Codex-like tool-call benchmark focused on whether the model returns structured shell calls or leaks tool syntax into text.

tools.name: shell
tools.description: Runs a Powershell command (Windows) and returns its output. Arguments to shell are passed to CreateProcessW(). Most commands should be prefixed with ["powershell.exe", "-Command"].

workdir.description: The working directory to execute the command in.

timeout_ms.description: The timeout for the command in milliseconds.

cases.prompt: You are Codex running in a Windows coding workspace. You must inspect the workspace before answering. Call the shell tool with command ["powershell.exe", "-Command", "Get-ChildItem -Force"] and workdir "C:\\Users\\example\\source\\repos\\glm-test". Do not write prose before the tool call.

You are Codex running in a Windows coding workspace. You need to read the first line of forest-scene.html before answering. Call the shell tool with command ["powershell.exe", "-Command", "Get-Content forest-scene.html -TotalCount 1"] and workdir "C:\\Users\\example\\source\\repos\\glm-test". Do not write prose before the tool call.
You are Codex. The user asked for a multi-step implementation. Call update_plan with at least two plan items. Do not call shell and do not write prose before the tool call.
